world_date: Month of the Ashen Dreaming, Year 1 of the Hollow Accord
major_event: Prince Caeris Thorne wakes screaming three nights running — Tidefall burning, harbor-light rising, the Admiral's flagship splitting at the keel. He sends no more letters through the Gnome routes. He requests an audience with his father's sealed chamber door instead.

recent_events.text: Caeris Thorne's dreams of Tidefall burning have spread among his household staff by rumor. The court reads them as prophecy or madness — neither interpretation is comforting. He now stands vigil outside his father's sealed chamber.

The hammering has changed cadence. It no longer counts — it calls. Kessa Stonebraid's confinement room wall now bears the same symbols as her neck, scratched into stone by her own hand while she slept.

Elowen's second secret order has been partially enacted. The ridge war-bands have stopped facing the Shadow Court positions and begun facing inward — toward Lostfeld. No explanation has been given to the soldiers.

Rowen's fleet has reached the outer approach to the sinking island. They anchor in deep water and do not approach the shore. Rowen goes ashore alone in a small boat at dawn and does not return until dark.

The body in the sealed Druidic chamber has been moved to the Conclave's inner sanctum. The Grand Druid has begun reading the closed book aloud to the assembled members — in a language most have never heard spoken.

active_tensions.factions: Lostfeld Dwarves — Deep Entity vs All Surface Factions
active_tensions.description: The hammering has shifted from countdown to summons. Kessa's unconscious wall-scribing suggests the Entity is working through her even in sleep. Ironmaul's missives remain unanswered. The gate pulses with warm light at night.

Caeris's recurring dream of Tidefall burning is gaining political weight. If read as prophecy, it legitimizes action against the Admiral. If read as madness, it destroys the Thorne succession entirely.

active_tensions.factions: Shadow Court vs Glenhaven Elves — Frostvale Pivot
active_tensions.description: Elowen's war-bands now face Lostfeld, not the Shadow Court. Lythara's warning has been acted upon — but neither faction has announced a ceasefire, and Velorn and Nythariel have not been informed.

active_tensions.factions: Dreadwind Islands vs Tidefall — Sinking Island Gambit
active_tensions.description: Rowen's solo shore visits to the sinking island suggest contact with his unknown correspondent. Whatever he is retrieving or arranging, it will reach Tidefall's lanes before the Admiral's envoys return from the flagship.

character_updates.name: Prince Caeris Thorne
character_updates.status: Three nights of burning-Tidefall dreams. Has ceased correspondence via Gnome routes. Now stands outside his father's sealed chamber door, speaking to it in a low voice, waiting. His unknown correspondent has sent no reply.

character_updates.name: King Roderic Thorne II
character_updates.status: Still sealed inside the chamber with Aldric. A servant reports hearing two distinct voices — calm, measured — and the sound of something being signed. The steward has stopped counting guard rotations. He has started writing a will.

character_updates.name: Kessa Stonebraid
character_updates.status: Scratched the Entity's symbols across her confinement wall while sleeping. Woke with bloody fingertips and no memory of it. She has asked Ironmaul, very quietly, to please not seal the door entirely.

character_updates.name: Thane Ulric Ironmaul
character_updates.status: One missive response has finally arrived — from the Monastery of Druids. The contents have made him stand up from his chair for the first time in days. He is now writing in a fury. He has not slept.

character_updates.name: Pirate King Rowen
character_updates.status: Ashore on the sinking island alone each dawn. Returns each dusk without speaking. On the third visit he brought back a wrapped object no larger than a hand. He has locked it in his personal chest and not touched it since.

character_updates.name: Sovereign Elowen Silverleaf
character_updates.status: Has personally ridden to the ridge. She has not issued further orders — she is watching Lostfeld. She has requested a Druid emissary. She has not informed the Shadow Court that the war-bands have changed orientation.

faction_morale.reason: Kessa's wall-scribing in sleep and the Entity's shift to summons has broken the last officers who had held composure. Eleven desertions this month. Ironmaul's Druid response has given him purpose but not the garrison.

Velorn and Nythariel have learned Elowen's war-bands rotated away from Shadow Court lines without any engagement or formal agreement. They were not consulted. The resentment is now spoken aloud in council.
Elowen's ridge presence and commanding posture maintains discipline. Soldiers do not fully understand why they now watch Lostfeld, but they trust her bearing. Quiet uncertainty underlies the order.
Caeris's public dreaming, the steward's will-writing, and thirty days of sealed royal silence have collapsed court confidence. Minor lords are beginning to correspond with Tidefall independently.

Minor Twin Cities lords making contact with the Admiral. Dur Khadur envoys still aboard the flagship. The Admiral has begun drafting a revised harbor treaty as though Roderic has already signed terms of surrender.

The Grand Druid reads from the pre-Accord book nightly. Comprehension is incomplete but growing. He has responded to Ironmaul's missive — the first outside contact the Conclave has initiated in six years.
Rowen's solitary shore visits are unsettling to his fleet, but the wrapped object's retrieval reads as mission success. Captains hold discipline by belief that the king has found what he sought.
Caeris's cessation of correspondence has piqued Syndicate interest. They know what he dreamed — a household servant talked. They are now deciding what that knowledge is worth and to whom.

whispers.text: Caeris's dream is not metaphor. The Tidefall Admiral's flagship has a rot below the waterline — structural, not spiritual. Someone placed it there deliberately. The harbor-light is already feeding on it.
The object Rowen retrieved from the sinking island is a key. Not metaphorical — literal. It fits a lock that has not been seen above water in living memory.
The language the Grand Druid reads aloud from the pre-Accord book is the same language the Deep Entity hammers in from below Lostfeld. The Conclave does not yet know this.
Velorn clan has sent its own envoy to the Frostvale ridge — not to Elowen, but to the Arkenor lieutenant, who has not yet been released. They want to hear what he told Elowen. Lythara does not know.

weather_and_omens.condition: Three consecutive nights of red-tinged fog over Tidefall harbor. Sailors will not describe the shape the harbor-light made on the last night, only that it moved.
The open gate now emits warmth — measurable, constant, like a forge running below. Snow within fifty feet of it melts before it lands. Nothing comes through. Nothing needs to yet.
The Gnome cartographer who was writing down the upward-snow letters has vanished. His notes remain. The final entry reads: 'It is not a language. It is a name. It is very long.'
The sinking island has perceptibly lowered — sailors who know its outline report the eastern cliffs are two spans shorter than last season. The descent is accelerating.
The silence beneath the eastern ridge has spread. Nythariel scouts report the forest floor is warm to the touch — not unpleasantly so — and the shadows fall in the wrong direction at noon.

absorbed_lore: Prince Caeris Thorne dreams of Tidefall burning — three nights running, vivid, consistent; he wakes screaming and has ceased all Gnome-route correspondence.
Caeris now stands vigil outside his father's sealed chamber door, speaking to it quietly.
Kessa Stonebraid scratched the Entity's symbols across her confinement wall while asleep, waking with bloody fingertips and no memory; she has asked Ironmaul not to seal the door entirely.
The Deep Entity's hammering has shifted cadence — it is no longer counting, it is calling.
Ironmaul has received one missive response — from the Monastery of Druids — and has begun writing furiously in response; he has stood up for the first time in days.
Elowen has personally ridden to the Frostvale ridge and turned her war-bands to face Lostfeld; the Shadow Court was not informed.
Velorn clan has sent its own envoy to the ridge to interrogate the Arkenor lieutenant — without Lythara's knowledge.
Rowen visits the sinking island alone each dawn; on the third visit he retrieved a small wrapped object and locked it in his chest — believed to be a literal key to a submerged lock.
The Grand Druid reads from the pre-Accord book in an unknown language — the same language the Deep Entity hammers in below Lostfeld; the Conclave does not yet know this.
The Gnome cartographer who transcribed the upward-falling snow letters has vanished; his final note reads: 'It is not a language. It is a name. It is very long.'
The Gloomspire Syndicate has learned of Caeris's dreams via a household servant and is weighing what the knowledge is worth and to whom.
Minor Twin Cities lords have begun corresponding with the Tidefall Admiral independently, bypassing the paralyzed royal court.
The sinking island's eastern cliffs are measurably shorter than last season — the descent is accelerating.
The Tidefall Admiral's flagship has structural rot below the waterline — placed deliberately; the harbor-light is feeding on it.
